author: sandeepkumar-skb
text: torch.inverse() performing very poorly on GPU vs CPU

author: sandeepkumar-skb
text:
## 🐛 Bug

We observed a big increase in inference latency after adding `torch.inverse()` in the code path. After investigation and comparison with moving the op to CPU we found that there is a huge difference in performance of that op on GPU vs CPU.  
The matrix size in our case is 4x4 which small for the GPU but `torch.inverse()` should be using `magma` library which has heuristics to move the op to CPU. We didn't see any magma invocations as well.

Based on [@ptrblck](https://github.com/ptrblck) suggestion we tried `inverse` on batched matrix to see if that made any difference but that also didn't result in any improvement.

**Performance Difference:**

```
╰─ python torch_inverse_exp.py
GPU Time: 8.78560 ms
CPU Time: 0.07149 ms
cpu/gpu: 122.90x
```

We increased the matrix dimensions to 1024x1024 but even then GPU is slower than CPU:

```
╰─ python torch_inverse_exp.py
GPU Time: 17.25289 ms
CPU Time: 9.54982 ms
cpu/gpu: 1.81x
```

## To Reproduce

Here is the snippet of code which can be run to reproduce the performance difference:

```
import torch
import time

a = torch.randn(4,4).cuda()
torch.cuda.synchronize()

num_iter = 100
def test1():
    s = time.time()
    for i in range(num_iter):
        x = torch.inverse(a)
    torch.cuda.synchronize()
    e = time.time()
    gpu_time = ((e - s)/num_iter)
    return  gpu_time

def test2():
    s = time.time()
    for i in range(num_iter):
        b = a.to('cpu')
        d = torch.inverse(b)
        y = d.to('cuda')
    torch.cuda.synchronize()
    e = time.time()
    cpu_time = ((e - s)/num_iter)
    return  cpu_time

gpu_time = test1()
print("GPU Time: {:.5f} ms".format(gpu_time*1000))

cpu_time = test2()
print("CPU Time: {:.5f} ms".format( cpu_time*1000))

print("cpu/gpu: {:.2f}x ".format(gpu_time/cpu_time))
```

**Run:** `python <script_name>`

author: ptrblck
text: [@xwang233](https://github.com/xwang233) could you try to grab the nsight profiles and check the call trace for these methods, please?

author: xwang233
text:
I can reproduce this slow GPU impl on CUDA 11 + magma 2.5.4 and CUDA 10.2 + magma 2.5.1.

Testing script

```
x = torch.randn(4, 4, device='cuda', dtype=torch.float)
with torch.autograd.profiler.emit_nvtx():
    y = torch.inverse(x)
```

nvprof nvtx results:

```
==3746000==       Range "aten::_inverse_helper, seq = 0"
            Type  Time(%)      Time     Calls       Avg       Min       Max  Name
          Range:  100.00%  1.56590s         1  1.56590s  1.56590s  1.56590s  aten::_inverse_helper, seq = 0
 GPU activities:   17.60%  7.2320us         7  1.0330us     800ns  1.3760us  [CUDA memcpy HtoD]
                   13.86%  5.6960us         1  5.6960us  5.6960us  5.6960us  strtri_diag_lower_kernel(magma_diag_t, int, float const *, int, float*)
                   11.14%  4.5760us         2  2.2880us  1.8560us  2.7200us  slacpy_full_kernel(int, int, float const *, int, float*, int)
                    8.26%  3.3920us         3  1.1300us  1.0560us  1.2480us  sswap_kernel(int, float*, int, float*, int)
                    7.55%  3.1040us         1  3.1040us  3.1040us  3.1040us  void gemmSN_NN_kernel<float, int=256, int=4, int=2, int=8, int=4, int=4, bool=0, cublasGemvTensorStridedBatched<float const >, cublasGemvTensorStridedBatched<float>>(cublasGemmSmallNParams<float const , cublasGemvTensorStridedBatched<float const >, float>)
                    7.17%  2.9440us         1  2.9440us  2.9440us  2.9440us  triple_sgemm16_part1_lower_kernel(int, float const *, int, float*, int, int)
                    7.09%  2.9120us         2  1.4560us  1.3760us  1.5360us  [CUDA memcpy DtoH]
                    6.39%  2.6240us         2  1.3120us  1.1520us  1.4720us  [CUDA memset]
                    6.31%  2.5920us         1  2.5920us  2.5920us  2.5920us  _ZN2at6native27unrolled_elementwise_kernelIZZZNS0_21copy_device_to_deviceERNS_14TensorIteratorEbENKUlvE0_clEvENKUlvE2_clEvEUlfE_NS_6detail5ArrayIPcLi2EEE16OffsetCalculatorILi1EjESC_NS0_6memory15LoadWithoutCastENSD_16StoreWithoutCastEEEviT_T0_T1_T2_T3_T4_
                    6.07%  2.4960us         1  2.4960us  2.4960us  2.4960us  triple_sgemm16_part2_lower_kernel(int, float const *, int, float*, int, int)
                    5.14%  2.1120us         1  2.1120us  2.1120us  2.1120us  slaset_lower_kernel(int, int, float, float, float*, int)
                    3.43%  1.4080us         1  1.4080us  1.4080us  1.4080us  slaset_full_kernel(int, int, float, float, float*, int)
      API calls:   45.73%  184.35us        12  15.362us  5.5090us  42.844us  cudaLaunchKernel
                   27.13%  109.36us         5  21.872us  15.111us  37.428us  cudaMemcpy
                   19.57%  78.875us         4  19.718us  12.290us  29.233us  cudaMemcpyAsync
                    5.04%  20.326us         1  20.326us  20.326us  20.326us  cudaMemsetAsync
                    2.53%  10.183us         1  10.183us  10.183us  10.183us  cudaMemset
```

It seems like there are some memcpy between host and device. I guess this code is the culprit

[pytorch/aten/src/ATen/native/cuda/BatchLinearAlgebra.cu](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/e98ad6c05b21f9ee04447de69e809af42f4e114a/aten/src/ATen/native/cuda/BatchLinearAlgebra.cu#L703-L705)

Lines 703 to 705
in
[e98ad6c](/pytorch/pytorch/commit/e98ad6c05b21f9ee04447de69e809af42f4e114a)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Tensor ipiv = at::empty({n}, at::kInt); |
|  | Tensor dwork = at::empty({lwork}, self.options()); |
|  | magmaLu<scalar\_t>(n, n, self\_data, n, ipiv.data\_ptr<magma\_int\_t>(), &info\_tmp); |

`Tensor ipiv` is initialized on CPU, and later passed to `magma_sgetrf_gpu` [[ref]](https://icl.cs.utk.edu/projectsfiles/magma/doxygen/group__magma__getrf.html#gae1751a9b0d4ca3137eb6fbcd28ea2d55) as an integer pointer. I tried modifying that to

```
Tensor ipiv = at::empty({n}, self.options().dtype(at::kInt));
```

but `magma_sgetrf_gpu` gives `segmentation fault (core dumped)` instead.

[@vishwakftw](https://github.com/vishwakftw) do you have time to take a look?

text: Add cusolver to build, rewrite MAGMA inverse with cusolver

author: mkolod
vishwakftw
mkolod
text: [@vishwakftw](https://github.com/vishwakftw) The issue is precisely because the code in question is being ported from TensorFlow 1.15.0, where the inverses for various matrix sizes run about 2 orders of magnitude faster. :)

author: Mapoet
text:
the reason to this issues is that too small rank of matrix to be solved, you can test with rank greater than 4000, it would show the significant improvement.  
the follow is what i modified and the result:

```
a = torch.randn(4000,4000).cuda()
torch.cuda.synchronize()
num_iter = 1
```

the result is:

```
GPU Time: 114.15434 ms
CPU Time: 460.38413 ms
cpu/gpu: 0.25x
```
